description: Ongoing discoveries test whether the next platform investments materially improve adoption, delivery flow, governance, and reliability for internal product and technology teams.

riskFramework.question: Will internal teams adopt and keep using the platform path?
Will the platform create enough leverage and risk reduction to justify continued investment?
Can teams understand and complete platform workflows without bespoke support?
Can the platform teams build and operate this reliably within current constraints?

items.title: Ephemeral environment economics and adoption discovery
items.name: Ephemeral environment economics and adoption discovery
items.summary: Discovery is testing where ephemeral environments create real flow benefits versus avoidable cost and operational complexity.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether expanding ephemeral environments across more teams improves delivery speed enough to justify cost, quota, and operational trade-offs.

linkedInitiatives.description: Runtime self-service and delivery initiative focused on improving environment provisioning and ephemeral environments, delivery pipelines and release automation, and runtime connectivity and integration patterns.

assignments.how: Leads the opportunity framing, platform instrumentation, and recommendation on where ephemeral environments should become a default.
Validates how ephemeral environments fit into the standard delivery path and rollback mechanics.
Provides usage, cost, and idle-spend evidence for the recommendation.
Contributes direct team feedback on workflow usability and operational fit.

riskFocus.question: Will internal teams adopt and keep using the platform path?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Mainstream product teams will only adopt ephemeral environments at scale when the flow benefits are obvious and the cleanup model is reliable.
evidenceToCollect: Whether individual software engineers actually keep using ephemeral environments after initial onboarding.
Which team workflows measurably speed up with ephemeral environments.
Where teams abandon or bypass the feature after initial trials.

riskFocus.question: Will the platform create enough leverage and risk reduction to justify continued investment?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Ephemeral environments are viable when they reduce waiting and defect cost more than they increase infrastructure spend and platform support burden.
evidenceToCollect: Environment cost and cleanup failure patterns.
Impact on test cycle time and defect escape.

items.title: Exception governance without ticket queues
items.name: Exception governance without ticket queues
items.summary: Discovery work is testing which policy exceptions should be automated, time-bound, and productized rather than handled through long-running manual review processes.
items.opportunity: Clarify how to keep governance strong while preventing exception handling from becoming a hidden platform bottleneck.

linkedInitiatives.description: Platform controls by default initiative focused on improving identity, policy, artifact, observability, and reliability workflows.

assignments.how: Leads discovery on policy types, evidence requirements, and exception workflow design.
Provides operational evidence about incidents and risks linked to missing or delayed controls.
Shows where policy checks and exception states should attach to the normal delivery workflow.
Brings direct team feedback on exception friction, confusion, and support needs.

riskFocus.question: Can teams understand and complete platform workflows without bespoke support?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Teams will comply more consistently when exception paths are explicit, fast, and understandable inside normal tooling.
evidenceToCollect: Which control failures ordinary software engineers can understand and resolve without escalation.
Where teams fail to understand current exception options.
How often manual governance interactions block delivery.

riskFocus.question: Can the platform teams build and operate this reliably within current constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Only the high-frequency and low-ambiguity exception classes should be productized first.
evidenceToCollect: Which exception classes recur often enough to automate.
Where ambiguity or risk remains too high for full self-service.
